standard.notes: Market staple in France. 'Un panier en osier' = a wicker basket. Shoppers at French outdoor markets ('marchés') typically carry a 'panier' rather than a plastic bag. 'Faire son marché avec un panier' is seen as an eco-friendly and traditional practice.
examples: Elle remplit son panier de légumes frais.
She fills her basket with fresh vegetables.
J'ai acheté un panier au marché.
I bought a basket at the market.

standard.meaning: the till / the checkout / the cashier's desk
standard.notes: 'Passer à la caisse' = to go to the checkout. 'La caissière / le caissier' = the cashier. In French markets, the 'caisse' is where you pay after the vendor weighs your produce. Also used in supermarkets.
examples: Vous payez à la caisse, s'il vous plaît.
You pay at the till, please.
Il y a la queue à la caisse.
There is a queue at the checkout.

standard.notes: Feminine form: 'fraîche'. 'Des légumes frais' = fresh vegetables. 'Des fraises fraîches' = fresh strawberries. Also means cool (temperature): 'Il fait frais ce matin' = It is cool this morning. A key selling word at French markets.
examples: Ces tomates sont très fraîches.
These tomatoes are very fresh.
Le poisson est frais ce matin.
The fish is fresh this morning.

standard.notes: Regular -er verb with a spelling change (è before silent endings). 'Pouvez-vous peser les pommes ?' = Can you weigh the apples? At French markets, produce is sold by weight (au kilo). 'La balance' = the scales used for weighing.
examples: Il pèse les pommes sur la balance.
He weighs the apples on the scales.
Pouvez-vous peser un kilo de cerises ?
Can you weigh a kilo of cherries?

standard.meaning: the aisle / the section / the department
standard.notes: 'Le rayon fruits et légumes' = the fruit and vegetable section. Used in supermarkets and large market halls to refer to specific sections. 'Le rayon boucherie' = the butcher's section. 'Quel rayon cherchez-vous ?' = Which section are you looking for?
examples: Les fromages sont au rayon crémerie.
The cheeses are in the dairy section.
Je cherche le rayon fruits et légumes.
I am looking for the fruit and vegetable section.

advanced.notes: Market staple in France. 'Un panier en osier' = a wicker basket. Shoppers at French outdoor markets ('marchés') typically carry a 'panier' rather than a plastic bag. 'Faire son marché avec un panier' is seen as an eco-friendly and traditional practice.
examples: Elle remplit son panier de légumes frais.
She fills her basket with fresh vegetables.
J'ai acheté un panier au marché.
I bought a basket at the market.

advanced.meaning: the till / the checkout / the cashier's desk
advanced.notes: 'Passer à la caisse' = to go to the checkout. 'La caissière / le caissier' = the cashier. In French markets, the 'caisse' is where you pay after the vendor weighs your produce. Also used in supermarkets.
examples: Vous payez à la caisse, s'il vous plaît.
You pay at the till, please.
Il y a la queue à la caisse.
There is a queue at the checkout.

examples: Ces tomates sont très fraîches.
These tomatoes are very fresh.
Le poisson est frais ce matin.
The fish is fresh this morning.

advanced.notes: Regular -er verb with a spelling change (è before silent endings). 'Pouvez-vous peser les pommes ?' = Can you weigh the apples? At French markets, produce is sold by weight (au kilo). 'La balance' = the scales used for weighing.
examples: Il pèse les pommes sur la balance.
He weighs the apples on the scales.
Pouvez-vous peser un kilo de cerises ?
Can you weigh a kilo of cherries?

advanced.meaning: the aisle / the section / the department
advanced.notes: 'Le rayon fruits et légumes' = the fruit and vegetable section. Used in supermarkets and large market halls to refer to specific sections. 'Le rayon boucherie' = the butcher's section. 'Quel rayon cherchez-vous ?' = Which section are you looking for?
examples: Les fromages sont au rayon crémerie.
The cheeses are in the dairy section.
Je cherche le rayon fruits et légumes.
I am looking for the fruit and vegetable section.

advanced.notes: 'Vous voulez un reçu ?' = Do you want a receipt? In French markets, receipts are less common for small cash purchases, but can always be requested. 'Garder son reçu' = to keep one's receipt. Essential for returns.
examples: Vous voulez un reçu ?
Do you want a receipt?
J'ai perdu mon reçu.
I have lost my receipt.

advanced.notes: 'Faire la file' or 'faire la queue' = to queue / to stand in line. 'La file d'attente' = the waiting queue. At busy French markets, queuing is expected and cutting in line ('resquiller') is frowned upon. 'Il y a une longue file' = there is a long queue.
examples: Il y a une longue file devant le stand.
There is a long queue in front of the stall.
Je fais la file depuis dix minutes.
I have been queuing for ten minutes.

dialogue: A
Moi, je prends les fruits — toi, tu prends le fromage, d'accord ?
B
D'accord ! Et lui, il s'occupe des légumes ?
A
Oui, c'est ça. Vous, vous pouvez peser les pommes, s'il vous plaît ?
vendeur
Bien sûr. Un kilo de pommes — voilà !
A
Merci. Et ces fraises — elles sont fraîches ?
vendeur
Très fraîches, madame. Ramassées ce matin.
B
Moi, je paye à la caisse — toi, tu gardes le panier ?
A
Oui ! On se retrouve là-bas dans cinq minutes.

questions.question: Who is in charge of buying the fruit?
What does the vendor confirm about the strawberries?
Who will pay at the till?

standard.summary: Two friends divide tasks at an outdoor market using emphatic pronouns: Speaker A takes the fruit, Speaker B takes the cheese, and a third person handles the vegetables. The vendor confirms the strawberries were picked that morning. Speaker B will pay at the till while Speaker A holds the basket.

dialogue: A
Moi, je commence par le rayon fromages — toi, tu t'occupes des fruits ?
B
Oui, moi je prends les pommes et les fraises. Elles sont fraîches ?
vendeur
Très fraîches — ramassées ce matin. Je vous pèse combien de kilos ?
B
Deux kilos de pommes, s'il vous plaît. Et lui, il arrive bientôt ?
A
Oui, lui il apporte les sacs. Nous, on n'a qu'un panier !
vendeur
Voilà votre reçu. Vous voulez passer à la caisse maintenant ?
B
Oui, moi je paye — toi, tu gardes notre place dans la file ?
A
D'accord. Eux, ils attendent aussi — la file est longue aujourd'hui !

questions.question: How many kilos of apples are ordered?
What does Speaker A note about their carrying situation?
options: They lost their place in the queue

questions.question: What will Speaker B do while Speaker A holds their place in the queue?

advanced.summary: Two friends split tasks at a busy outdoor market using emphatic pronouns throughout. Speaker A handles the cheese section while Speaker B buys fruit (two kilos of apples). A third friend is bringing bags. Speaker B will pay at the till while Speaker A holds their place in the long queue.

standard.title: Stressed / Emphatic Pronouns (moi, toi, lui, elle, nous, vous, eux, elles)
standard.explanation:
**Stressed pronouns** (also called emphatic or disjunctive pronouns) are a separate set of pronouns used in specific situations — they are never the main subject of a verb.

**The full set:**
| Subject pronoun | Stressed pronoun |
|---|---|
| je | **moi** |
| tu | **toi** |
| il | **lui** |
| elle | **elle** |
| nous | **nous** |
| vous | **vous** |
| ils | **eux** |
| elles | **elles** |

**When to use stressed pronouns:**

1. **After prepositions** (avec, pour, chez, sans, etc.)
 - *C'est pour moi.* = It's for me.
 - *Je viens avec toi.* = I'm coming with you.

2. **For emphasis or contrast**
 - *Moi, j'aime le marché. Toi, tu préfères le supermarché.*
 - = I like the market. You prefer the supermarket.

3. **In comparisons**
 - *Elle est plus grande que moi.* = She is taller than me.

4. **Alone or in short answers**
 - *Qui veut du fromage ? — Moi !* = Who wants cheese? — Me!

5. **With 'c'est'**
 - *C'est lui.* = It's him. *C'est elles.* = It's them.

examples.english: I'll take the fruit. (emphatic — contrast with others)

examples.french: Elle est plus grande que lui.

drills.question: Complete: C'est pour ___ ? (Is it for me?)
drills.explanation: After prepositions like 'pour', use stressed pronouns. 'Pour moi' = for me.

drills.question: Which is correct? 'She is taller than ___.'
drills.explanation: In comparisons, use stressed pronouns after 'que'. 'Il' becomes 'lui'.

drills.question: Complete: ___, je prends le fromage. (I'll take the cheese. — emphatic)
drills.explanation: 'Moi' at the start of a sentence gives emphasis or contrast.

drills.question: How do you say 'Who wants the basket? — Them! (feminine)'?
drills.explanation: For a group of women/feminine, use 'elles' as a stressed pronoun.

advanced.title: Stressed / Emphatic Pronouns — All Uses, Contrast & Fixed Expressions
advanced.explanation:
**Stressed pronouns** (pronoms toniques / disjoints) stand apart from the main verb. They appear in a range of grammatical and stylistic contexts.

**Full set:**
| Subject | Stressed |
|---|---|
| je | **moi** |
| tu | **toi** |
| il | **lui** |
| elle | **elle** |
| nous | **nous** |
| vous | **vous** |
| ils | **eux** |
| elles | **elles** |

**All major uses:**

**1. After prepositions** (chez, avec, pour, sans, devant, derrière, etc.)
- *chez moi* = at my place | *sans elle* = without her | *devant eux* = in front of them

**2. Emphasis / contrast** (often with a comma)
- *Moi, je travaille. Lui, il reste.* = I'm working. He's staying.
- *Toi, tu exagères !* = You're exaggerating!

**3. Compound subjects** (two or more subjects)
- *Toi et moi, nous partons.* = You and I are leaving.
- *Elle et lui arrivent ensemble.* = She and he are arriving together.

**4. Comparisons**
- *Il court plus vite que moi.* = He runs faster than me.
- *Elle est aussi intelligente que lui.* = She is as intelligent as him.

**5. Alone / short answers**
- *Qui fait la vaisselle ? — Moi !* = Who does the dishes? — Me!

**6. With 'c'est / ce sont'**
- *C'est moi / toi / lui / elle / nous / vous.* = It's me/you/him…
- *Ce sont eux / elles.* = It's them. (plural: use 'ce sont')

**7. With '-même(s)' for reflexive emphasis**
- *Je le fais moi-même.* = I'm doing it myself.
- *Ils l'ont construit eux-mêmes.* = They built it themselves.

**Common fixed expressions:**
- *Chez moi* = at my place | *Chez toi* = at your place
- *Entre nous* = between us | *À toi de jouer* = your turn
- *Quant à moi* = as for me | *Pour eux* = for them

examples.french: Moi, je prends les fruits. Toi, tu prends le fromage.
examples.english: I'll take the fruit. You'll take the cheese. (contrast)

examples.french: C'est elle qui paye — pas moi !
examples.english: It's her who's paying — not me!

examples.french: Ce sont eux qui attendent dans la file.
examples.english: It's them (they) who are waiting in the queue.

drills.question: Which stressed pronoun is correct? 'It's them (a mixed group).'
drills.explanation: For plural 'them', use 'ce sont' (not 'c'est') + eux (mixed/masculine) or elles (all feminine).

drills.question: Complete: Je le fais ___-même. (I'm doing it myself.)
drills.explanation: '-même' attaches to the stressed pronoun: moi-même = myself, toi-même = yourself, etc.

drills.question: Choose the correct form: 'Between you and me, the queue is long.'
drills.explanation: After the preposition 'entre', use stressed pronouns: toi et moi.

drills.question: Complete: À ___ de jouer ! (Your turn! — singular informal)
drills.explanation: 'À toi de jouer' = your turn (informal singular). 'Toi' is the stressed form of 'tu'.

drills.question: Which sentence uses an emphatic pronoun for contrast correctly?
options: Moi, je reste. Lui, il part.
Je reste, moi. Il part, lui.
Je moi reste. Il lui part.
drills.correctAnswer: Moi, je reste. Lui, il part.
drills.explanation: For emphasis/contrast, the stressed pronoun comes first (before the subject + verb): Moi, je… / Lui, il…

drills.question: Complete: Elle court plus vite que ___. (She runs faster than him.)
drills.explanation: In comparisons after 'que', use stressed pronouns. 'Il' → 'lui' in stressed position.

drills.question: How do you say 'They built it themselves (all female)'?
drills.explanation: '-même' agrees with the subject: elles → elles-mêmes (plural feminine).

drills.question: Complete: C'est ___ qui apporte le panier. (It's her who's bringing the basket.)
drills.explanation: After 'c'est … qui', use the stressed pronoun: c'est elle qui… = it's her who…

standard.question: What does 'le panier' mean?
standard.explanation: 'Le panier' = the basket. Used for carrying shopping at a French outdoor market.

standard.question: Which stressed pronoun is the emphatic form of 'ils'?
standard.explanation: 'Eux' is the stressed/emphatic form of 'ils' (masculine plural).

standard.question: Complete: C'est pour ___, ce fromage ? (Is this cheese for me?)
standard.explanation: After 'pour', use a stressed pronoun. 'Moi' = me (stressed form of 'je').

standard.question: What does 'peser' mean?
standard.explanation: 'Peser' = to weigh. At a French market, the vendor 'pèse' the produce before you pay.

standard.question: What does 'frais / fraîche' mean?
standard.explanation: 'Frais' (m) / 'fraîche' (f) = fresh or cool. 'Des légumes frais' = fresh vegetables.

advanced.question: What does 'la file d'attente' mean?
advanced.explanation: 'La file d'attente' = the waiting queue. 'Faire la file' = to queue.

advanced.question: Which sentence correctly uses 'ce sont' with a stressed pronoun?
advanced.explanation: With plural emphasis, use 'ce sont' + stressed pronoun: ce sont eux (not c'est eux).

advanced.question: Complete: Ils l'ont pesé ___-mêmes. (They weighed it themselves — masculine.)
advanced.explanation: 'Eux-mêmes' = themselves (masculine plural). '-même' always attaches to the stressed pronoun.

advanced.question: What does 'le reçu' mean?
advanced.explanation: 'Le reçu' = the receipt. 'Vous voulez un reçu ?' = Do you want a receipt?

advanced.question: Complete: Quant à ___, je préfère les marchés. (As for me, I prefer markets.)
advanced.explanation: 'Quant à moi' = as for me. A fixed expression using the stressed pronoun 'moi'.

advanced.question: How do you say 'without her' in French?
advanced.explanation: After prepositions like 'sans', use stressed pronouns. 'Elle' = stressed form for 'elle' (singular feminine).

standard.scenario: You are at an outdoor market with a friend. Discuss who will buy what using emphatic pronouns (moi, toi, lui, elle). Order some fruit by weight and ask where to pay.
keyPhrases: Moi, je prends… / Toi, tu prends… (I'll take… / You'll take…)
Un kilo de… s'il vous plaît. (A kilo of… please.)
Je paye à la caisse ? (Do I pay at the till?)
standard.modelAnswer: Moi, je prends les fruits et toi, tu prends le fromage. Bonjour ! Pouvez-vous peser un kilo de pommes et un demi-kilo de fraises ? Elles sont fraîches ? Parfait. Et je paye à la caisse ?
standard.modelAnswerExplanation: The answer opens with emphatic pronouns for task allocation, then addresses the vendor directly using 'vous', requests specific weights, asks about freshness using 'fraîches', and ends by asking about payment.
standard.tip: Emphatic pronouns come at the start of a sentence for contrast: 'Moi, je…' and 'Toi, tu…' — the subject pronoun still follows!

advanced.scenario: At a busy outdoor market, negotiate with your friend who buys what, using emphatic pronouns throughout (including '-même' and 'c'est … qui'). Order produce by weight and discuss the queue and receipt.
keyPhrases: Moi, je m'occupe de… / Toi, tu t'occupes de… (I'll handle… / You'll handle…)
C'est elle qui paye — pas moi ! (It's her who pays — not me!)
Je le fais moi-même. (I'll do it myself.)
Pouvez-vous me peser deux kilos de… ? (Can you weigh me two kilos of…?)
La file est longue — garde notre place ! (The queue is long — hold our spot!)
advanced.modelAnswer: Moi, je m'occupe du fromage — toi, tu t'occupes des fruits. C'est toi qui pèses les légumes, pas moi ! Bonjour — pouvez-vous me peser deux kilos de tomates ? Elles sont fraîches ce matin ? Vous pouvez me donner un reçu, s'il vous plaît ? La file est longue — lui, il garde notre place pendant que moi, je passe à la caisse.
advanced.modelAnswerExplanation: The answer uses moi/toi/lui for task allocation, the 'c'est … qui' construction for emphasis, -même implied in 'moi, je passe', requests specific weights, confirms freshness, asks for a receipt, and uses 'pendant que' for simultaneous actions.
